issues.title: [Backend] Network Context Middleware - Extract and Validate X-Stellar-Network Header
issues.body:
## 🎯 Problem Statement

The backend is currently hardcoded to a single Stellar network with no way for clients to specify testnet vs mainnet at runtime. This prevents:
- Mobile clients from switching networks
- Testing against testnet without separate deployments
- Multi-network support in a single backend instance

## 💡 Solution

Create an Axum middleware that extracts the `X-Stellar-Network` HTTP header, validates it, and injects network context into all downstream handlers.

## 📁 Files to Create/Modify

### New Files
```
backend/src/middleware/network_context.rs
backend/src/models/network.rs
backend/src/middleware/mod.rs
```

### Modified Files
```
backend/src/main.rs
backend/src/lib.rs
backend/Cargo.toml
```

## 🔧 Technical Implementation

### NetworkContext Struct
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub enum StellarNetwork {
    Testnet,
    Mainnet,
}

impl FromStr for StellarNetwork {
    type Err = NetworkError;
    
    fn from_str(s: &str) -> Result<Self, Self::Err> {
        match s.to_lowercase().as_str() {
            "testnet" => Ok(Self::Testnet),
            "mainnet" => Ok(Self::Mainnet),
            _ => Err(NetworkError::InvalidNetwork(s.to_string())),
        }
    }
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct NetworkContext {
    pub network: StellarNetwork,
    pub raw_header_value: String,
}
```

### Middleware Implementation
```rust
pub async fn network_context_middleware(
    mut req: Request<Body>,
    next: Next<Body>,
) -> Result<Response, StatusCode> {
    // Extract header
    let network_header = req
        .headers()
        .get("X-Stellar-Network")
        .and_then(|h| h.to_str().ok());
    
    // Validate and parse
    let network = match network_header {
        Some(value) => {
            StellarNetwork::from_str(value)
                .map_err(|_| StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST)?
        }
        None => {
            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]
            {
                tracing::warn!("No X-Stellar-Network header, defaulting to testnet");
                StellarNetwork::Testnet
            }
            #[cfg(not(debug_assertions))]
            {
                tracing::error!("Missing required X-Stellar-Network header");
                return Err(StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST);
            }
        }
    };
    
    // Create context
    let context = NetworkContext {
        network,
        raw_header_value: network_header.unwrap_or("testnet").to_string(),
    };
    
    // Log with request ID
    tracing::info!(
        network = ?context.network,
        "Request network context"
    );
    
    // Inject into extensions
    req.extensions_mut().insert(context);
    
    // Continue to next middleware/handler
    Ok(next.run(req).await)
}
```

### Error Response Format
```json
{
  "error": "invalid_network",
  "message": "X-Stellar-Network header must be 'testnet' or 'mainnet'",
  "received": "invalid-value",
  "valid_values": ["testnet", "mainnet"]
}
```

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria

### Functional Requirements
- [ ] Middleware extracts `X-Stellar-Network` header from all requests
- [ ] Validates header value is exactly "testnet" or "mainnet" (case-insensitive)
- [ ] Returns HTTP 400 with JSON error body for invalid network values
- [ ] Returns HTTP 400 if header is missing in production mode
- [ ] Defaults to "testnet" if header is missing in development mode only
- [ ] Injects `NetworkContext` struct into Axum request extensions
- [ ] NetworkContext is accessible in all downstream handlers via `Extension<NetworkContext>`
- [ ] Middleware is applied globally to all API routes

### Logging & Observability
- [ ] Logs network context with every request using structured logging
- [ ] Includes request ID in network context logs for tracing
- [ ] Adds `network` label to all request metrics (e.g., `http_requests_total{network="testnet"}`)
- [ ] Logs warning when defaulting to testnet in dev mode
- [ ] Logs error when header is missing in production mode

### Code Quality
- [ ] Follows Rust best practices and idioms
- [ ] No `unwrap()` or `expect()` calls - proper error handling throughout
- [ ] Comprehensive rustdoc comments on all public items
- [ ] Unit tests achieve >80% code coverage
- [ ] Integration tests with real HTTP requests
- [ ] Clippy passes with no warnings
- [ ] Code formatted with rustfmt

### Error Handling
- [ ] Returns proper HTTP status codes (400 for bad requests)
- [ ] Error responses are JSON formatted
- [ ] Error messages are clear and actionable
- [ ] Errors include context (what was received, what's expected)

## 🧪 Testing Strategy

### Unit Tests
```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;
    
    #[test]
    fn test_parse_testnet() {
        assert_eq!(StellarNetwork::from_str("testnet").unwrap(), StellarNetwork::Testnet);
        assert_eq!(StellarNetwork::from_str("TESTNET").unwrap(), StellarNetwork::Testnet);
        assert_eq!(StellarNetwork::from_str("TeStNeT").unwrap(), StellarNetwork::Testnet);
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_parse_mainnet() {
        assert_eq!(StellarNetwork::from_str("mainnet").unwrap(), StellarNetwork::Mainnet);
        assert_eq!(StellarNetwork::from_str("MAINNET").unwrap(), StellarNetwork::Mainnet);
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_parse_invalid() {
        assert!(StellarNetwork::from_str("invalid").is_err());
        assert!(StellarNetwork::from_str("").is_err());
        assert!(StellarNetwork::from_str("test").is_err());
    }
}
```

### Integration Tests
- Test middleware in full request pipeline
- Test that NetworkContext is accessible in handlers
- Test error responses are properly formatted
- Test case-insensitive header matching
- Test missing header behavior in dev vs prod

### Performance Tests
- Middleware should add <1ms latency per request
- No memory leaks with repeated requests
- Header extraction is O(1) operation

## 📊 Performance Considerations

- Header extraction: O(1) HashMap lookup
- String parsing: Single allocation for lowercase conversion
- No database or external API calls
- Minimal memory overhead (<100 bytes per request)
- Target: <0.5ms overhead per request

## 🔒 Security Considerations

- Validate and sanitize all header values
- Prevent header injection attacks
- Log suspicious network switching patterns
- Rate limit requests with invalid headers
- Don't expose internal error details in production

## 📚 Documentation Requirements

- [ ] Rustdoc comments on all public items
- [ ] API documentation updated with header requirements
- [ ] Example requests showing header usage
- [ ] Error response documentation
- [ ] Migration guide for existing clients

## 🔗 Dependencies

**Depends on**: None (foundational change)

**Required by**: 
- Issue #2 (Database Schema Separation)
- Issue #3 (Network-Aware RPC Client)
- All other backend issues

## ⏱️ Estimated Effort

**Total: 3-5 hours**

- Implementation: 1.5 hours
- Unit tests: 1 hour
- Integration tests: 1 hour
- Documentation: 0.5 hours
- Code review & refinement: 1 hour

## ✔️ Definition of Done

- [ ] Code implemented and compiles without warnings
- [ ] All unit tests pass (>80% coverage)
- [ ] All integration tests pass
- [ ] Clippy passes with no warnings
- [ ] Code formatted with rustfmt
- [ ] Rustdoc documentation complete
- [ ] Code reviewed and approved by team
- [ ] Merged to main branch
- [ ] Deployed to staging environment
- [ ] Verified working in staging
